about.title: About RCT Labs
about.subtitle: Building an enterprise AI architecture program around a 10-layer model, a 41-algorithm framework, and sovereign data control.
about.mission_p1: We are committed to building AI systems that are constitutionally sound — where every output is verified through multi-LLM consensus, cryptographically signed, and fully auditable. Our 10-Layer architecture ensures trust at every level.
about.mission_p2: Through the JITNA Protocol (RFC-001 v2.0), SignedAI consensus, and a 41-algorithm framework, we're building the Constitutional AI Operating System with benchmark-driven verification and staged deployment maturity.
about.vision_p1: A future where every AI decision is verifiable, every output is signed, and every interaction respects regional compliance and data sovereignty across 8 markets.
about.vision_p2: We envision enterprise AI infrastructure operated to a 99.98% uptime SLA, stronger cost efficiency, and Constitutional AI guardrails grounded in benchmark evidence rather than marketing-only claims.
about.value_constitutional_desc: Every output should be verified through multi-LLM consensus with ED25519 cryptographic signatures and benchmark-backed quality controls
about.value_architecture_desc: 10-layer model with 6 Kernel RFCs, a 7 Genome System, and a 41-algorithm framework aligned to staged engineering rollout
about.value_regional_desc: 8 language-region markets with PDPA, APPI, PIPA, PIPL compliance and data locality
about.value_enterprise_desc: March 2026 public snapshot: 62 runtime components, 4,849 passing tests, 7 HexaCore models, and enterprise deployment evidence under active publication
about.team_subtitle: A solo-built platform led by Ittirit Saengow with editorial review support from the RCT Research Desk
about.journey_subtitle: Key milestones in RCT Labs' evolution
about.cta_subtitle: We're always looking for talented researchers, engineers, and builders to help shape the future of AI.

philosophy.title: RCT Philosophy
philosophy.subtitle: Explore the foundational concepts and frameworks that power the Intent Operating System
philosophy.fdia_desc: F = (D^I) × A framework that defines the relationship between Data, Intent, and Action
philosophy.rct7_desc: A seven-step implementation methodology for building intent-driven systems
philosophy.jitna_desc: Just-In-Time Natural Action language for precise intent specification
philosophy.intentos_desc: The operating system of the future built on intent-driven principles
philosophy.revolution_p1: RCT Labs is pioneering a fundamental shift in how humans and AI interact. Rather than following rigid procedures or isolated instructions, we envision systems that understand and align with human intent at the deepest level.
philosophy.revolution_p2: Our philosophy centers on four interconnected pillars: the mathematical rigor of the FDIA formula, the practical implementation of the RCT-7 process, the linguistic precision of JITNA, and the architectural vision of Intent OS.
philosophy.revolution_p3: Together, these frameworks form a complete paradigm for building AI systems that are not just intelligent, but intentional—capable of understanding what users truly want and acting accordingly with transparency and alignment.
philosophy.cta_subtitle: Dive deep into our research, join our community, and help shape the future of AI.

fdia.title: FDIA Formula
fdia.subtitle: F = (D^I) × A: The mathematical foundation of intent-driven AI systems
fdia.formula_desc: Intent Operating System = (Data raised to Intent power) multiplied by Action
fdia.d_desc: The raw material of understanding. All observable patterns, contexts, and historical information that informs decision-making.
fdia.i_desc: The exponential multiplier of understanding. Intent transforms raw data into purposeful knowledge by defining what truly matters.
fdia.a_desc: The actualization layer. The tangible output that manifests intention in the real world, guided by the Data-Intent relationship.
fdia.why_amplification_desc: The exponent in the formula (D^I) represents how intent exponentially amplifies the value of data. Without clear intent, data is just noise. With precise intent, data becomes pure signal.
fdia.why_scalable_desc: This formula scales from individual tasks to complex organizational systems. Whether optimizing a single decision or orchestrating an entire intelligence ecosystem, FDIA provides the mathematical foundation.
fdia.why_measurable_desc: By quantifying the relationship between data, intent, and action, we can measure and optimize for true alignment between AI systems and human values.
fdia.next_subtitle: Discover how to implement FDIA principles through our seven-step methodology for building intent-driven systems.

rct7.title: RCT-7 Process
rct7.subtitle: Seven-step methodology for implementing intent-driven systems
rct7.step_1_desc: Identify and clarify the core intent behind a problem or task
rct7.step_1_details: Extract the fundamental goal, values, and constraints that drive decision-making. What truly matters in this context?
rct7.step_2_desc: Gather and understand all relevant contextual information
rct7.step_2_details: Collect historical data, patterns, and environmental signals. Create a comprehensive picture of the situation.
rct7.step_3_desc: Align data patterns with intentional outcomes
rct7.step_3_details: Create explicit connections between available data and desired intent. Define the relationship matrix.
rct7.step_4_desc: Design the system to compute F = (D^I) × A
rct7.step_4_details: Build the computational model that transforms data through intent to generate aligned actions.
rct7.step_5_desc: Deploy the intent-driven system into production
rct7.step_5_details: Integrate with existing systems, configure parameters, and establish feedback mechanisms.
rct7.step_6_desc: Verify that outputs align with original intent
rct7.step_6_details: Run comprehensive tests to ensure actions generated are truly aligned with stated intentions.
rct7.step_7_desc: Monitor, learn, and improve the system over time
rct7.step_7_details: Gather feedback, adjust parameters, and evolve the system to better understand and serve intent.
rct7.individuals_point_1: Clarify your personal or professional intent
rct7.next_subtitle: Learn how to specify intent precisely using our Just-In-Time Natural Action language.

jitna.title: JITNA Language
jitna.subtitle: Just-In-Time Natural Action language for precise intent specification
jitna.feature_1_desc: Written in plain English that both humans and machines can understand. No arcane syntax or cryptic notation.
jitna.feature_2_desc: Clear sections for intent, context, priorities, and validation. Every element serves a purpose in specification.
jitna.feature_3_desc: Combine simple intent statements into complex, hierarchical specifications. Modular and reusable by design.
jitna.feature_4_desc: Every JITNA statement includes validation criteria. Actions can be measured against original intent.
jitna.example_1_content:
INTENT: Provide personalized product recommendations
CONTEXT: User browsing history, purchase patterns
PRIORITY: Relevance > Diversity > Profitability
ACTION: Generate top 5 recommendations
VALIDATE: CTR > baseline AND diversity score > 0.7
jitna.example_2_content:
INTENT: Maintain community safety while preserving expression
CONTEXT: User reports, content policies, community norms
PRIORITY: Prevent harm > Minimize false positives
ACTION: Flag or remove violating content
VALIDATE: False positive rate < 5% AND incident response < 2h
jitna.why_1_desc: Close the distance between human intent and machine execution. JITNA ensures nothing is lost in translation.
jitna.why_2_desc: Explicit validation criteria create measurable outcomes. We can prove whether systems are truly aligned with intent.
jitna.why_3_desc: Humans and AI collaborate using the same language. Engineers, managers, and stakeholders all speak JITNA.
jitna.why_4_desc: By making intent explicit, we can identify and correct misalignments before they compound into systemic problems.
jitna.next_subtitle: Discover the future operating system built entirely on intent-driven principles.
